title: coreopsis poloe
intro: Coreopsis poloe, also known as the golden ragwort or tickseed, is a flowering plant species native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a popular remedy for wound healing and skin issues. The health benefits of Coreopsis poloe include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable herb in herbal medicine. Coreopsis poloe can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its benefits.
